REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, NEGLIGENCE AND NEGLIGENCE PER SE, SELF-HELP EVICTION, NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF COVENANT OF QUIET ENJOYMENT ARE STRICKEN FROM THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF'S TIME TO AMEND THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION LAPSED IN APRIL 2022. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SEEK LEAVE TO AMEND BEFORE FILING FIST AMENDED COMPLAINT ON DECEMBER 20, 2023. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL RAISED FOR THE REPLY IS NOT CONSIDERED. ANY SUCH REQUEST SHALL BE MADE A PROPERLY NOTICED MOTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC. AND ARLENE HENRY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY. THIS IS AN AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE TO AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED ON THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNTENANTABLE DWELLING, AS THE PLAINTIFF ALLEGES FACTS THAT SHOW DEFENDANT'S NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CAL. CIV. CODE SECTION 1941.1. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION AS PLAINTIFF ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO CONSTITUTE A BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE DEMURRER TO THE UCL CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT SHOW THE REQUIRED ELEMENTS OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION HAVE BEEN MET. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-12-2023 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And Supporting Memorandum By Defendants Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Arlene Henry
Off Calendar Apr-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Vernon C. Goins II; Law Offices of Vernon C. Goins; 510-663-3700; appearing for defendants Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. and Arlene Henry.
Mark Smith; 415-410-8792; plaintiff, appearing in pro per.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC, ARLENE HENRY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And Supporting Memorandum By Defendants Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Arlene Henry is GRANTED. No opposition on file.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2023 Continued To May-22-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR, moot. Motion to Strike the operative complaint granted and order entered on March 24, 2023.
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And Supporting Memorandum By Defendants Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Arlene Henry
Off Calendar Apr-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC, ARLENE HENRY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And Supporting Memorandum By Defendants Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Arlene Henry is DENIED, without prejudice. The motion was filed prematurely, prior to the entry of order striking the First Amended Complaint. (CCP 581(f)(3).) Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-17-2023 Continued To Apr-14-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is CONTINUED to April 14, 2023 for supplemental briefing to address whether this motion is moot in light of the filing of the first amended complaint. (State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) Moving party shall submit a supplemental brief not to exceed two pages no later than March 29, 2023, opposing party may respond (brief not to exceed two pages) by no later than April 5, 2023. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendants Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS' AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Causes of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress, Negligence and Negligence per Se, self-help eviction, negligence and breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment are stricken from the First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's time to amend those causes of action lapsed in April 2022. Plaintiff failed to seek leave to amend before filing Fist Amended Complaint on December 20, 2023. Defendant's request for dismissal raised for the reply is not considered. Any such request shall be made a properly noticed motion.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendants Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-17-2023 Continued To Mar-03-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS' AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to March 3, 2023 on Court's own motion.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC AND ARLENE HENRY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR, re-noticed for February 17, 2023. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendant Arlene Henry'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum; And Mister Phillips' Declaration
Off Calendar Dec-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC AND ARLENE HENRY'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of causes of action as to defendant Arlene Henry for untenantable dwelling, breach of contract and UCL violation. The Court is further inclined to reconsider its decision to sustain the demurrer to the cause of action for breach of warranty of habitability. If parties do not contest this portion of the tentative ruling, it will become the order of the Court. If parties contest this portion of the ruling, the Court will set a hearing and allow for supplemental briefing as to the sole issue of reconsidering the Court's decision to sustain the demurrer "without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of warranty of habitability. This is an affirmative defense to an unlawful detainer action." Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendant Arlene Henry'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum; And Mister Phillips' Declaration
Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Continued To Dec-01-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC, ARLENE HENRY Defendant Arlene Henry'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is continued to December 1, 2022 for parties to provide courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to this motion no later than November 10, 2022 with cover letters reflecting new hearing date. (reply received). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2022 Set For Trial On Apr-17-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARK SMITH's DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, facts sufficiently stated to support affirmative defenses. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARK SMITH's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT is DENIED. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2022 Continued To Oct-05-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2022 Continued To Aug-03-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC. AND ARLENE HENRY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first Cause of Action for Breach of Implied Warranty of Habitability. This is an affirmative defense to an unlawful detainer action. The demurrer is overruled on the seventh cause of action for untenantable dwelling, as the Plaintiff alleges facts that show Defendant's noncompliance with Cal. Civ. Code section 1941.1. The Demurrer is overruled as to the eighth cause of action as Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to constitute a breach of contract. The demurrer to the UCL cause of action is overruled. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend on the remaining causes of action for plaintiff to allege facts that show the required elements of each cause of action have been met.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEFENDANTS JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC and ARLENE HENRY'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is Transferred to be heard in department 501 at 9:30 a.m. on March 10, 2022. That department handles all landlord-tenant/habitability cases per SF Local Rule 8.10A1.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2022 Continued To Mar-23-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Mark Smith (415.410.8792) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Vernon Goins, Esq. (510.663.3700; vgoins@goinslawfirm.com) for Defendants.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC and ARLENE HENRY'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT:

Defendants' demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which law requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer may be filed. Defendants' response to the complaint is now due by February 2, 2022.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
